onset_beat.description: A continuous offset value measured in metrical beats whose durations depend on the denominators of the respective time signatures (and a measure's actual duration).

tpc.description: Tonal Pitch Class (0=C, -1=F, 1=G, etc., aka specific pitch, aka fifths)

beat_float.description: The decimal beat position of any event within the current measure. A measure in n/m meter is considered to consist of n beats that have duration of 1/m of a whole note; except if n is a multiple of 3 (6, 9, 12, but not 3), in which case a measure is considered to have n/3 beats of length 3/m whole notes. All positions that fall in between downbeats are scaled linearly. For example, the first for eigths in a 9/8 meter have beat floats 1, 1.333, 1.667, 2. Values are rounded to three decimals.

downbeat.description: Adopts the integer values from beat_float and the value 0 for the rest.

is_downbeat.description: Indicates whether an event falls on a downbeat or not. Is False when downbeat == 0, True otherwise.

staff.description: Number of the staff containing the note, 1 being the upper staff

duration.description: Note duration expressed as fraction of a whole note

is_note_onset.description: False when a note is tied to a previous one

mc.description: Measure count, ID of the measure-like object (non-unique in unfolded score)
mc.used_for: input graph metadata and informational purposes

mc_playthrough.description: Measure count, unique in unfolded score
mc_playthrough.used_for: input graph metadata and informational purposes

mn.description: Measure number as per conventions. One MN can be composed of several MC.
mn.used_for: input graph metadata and informational purposes

mn_playthrough.description: Conventional measure numbers but for unfolded score (means of identifying complete measures)

octave.description: Octave of the note with 4 = middle octave. Does not always correspond to pitch // 12 - 1.
octave.used_for: input graph metadata and informational purposes

section_start.description: True for notes on the first position following a double/repeat bar line or section break. True values always correspond to the beginning of an MC, so a section beginning with a rest will not be taken into account.

a_isOnset.description: True for notes coinciding with a change in harmony.

cadence.description: Original cadence label (my include cadence subtypes)

cadence_type.description: Cadence label ∈ (PAC, IAC, HC, EC, DC, PC)

a_phraseend.description: True for all notes that coincide with the structural ending of a phrase (i.e., the phrase can have a codetta after this position before the next one begins).

unfolded_harmony_index.description: Index of the labels in the original unfolded table (before merging it with the notes)

globalkey_tpc.description: Root of the global key expressed as Tonal Pitch Class (0=C, -1=F, 1=G, etc., aka specific pitch, aka fifths)

localkey_tpc.description: Root of the local key expressed as Tonal Pitch Class (0=C, -1=F, 1=G, etc., aka specific pitch, aka fifths)

tonicized_tpc.description: Root of the tonicized key expressed as Tonal Pitch Class (0=C, -1=F, 1=G, etc., aka specific pitch, aka fifths)

sic_with_local.description: Relative position of the note's tonal pitch class in the local key, expressed as Specific Interval Class (0=unison, -1=+P4/-P5, 3=+M6/-m3, etc.)

tpc_is_in_label.description: True if a note's pitch class is part of the harmony label

tpc_is_root.description: True if a note's tonal pitch class is the harmony label's root

tpc_is_bass.description: True if a note's tonal pitch class is the harmony label's bass

a_degree1.description: Chordal root expressed as scale degree, preceeded by '#' for sharps and '-' for flats,as music21 would output them. For applied chords, such as #vii/vi, the scale degree represents the numeral before the slash; the second part will be represented in a_degree2.

a_degree2.description: Only defined for applied chords where this value expresses the tonicized key as a scale degree of the localkey in vigour. The format is the same as for a_degree1.

a_quality.description: This is a mapping of the chord_type column to music21's vocabulary for chord qualities.

valid_chord_label.description: True when a valid chord label is available for a note.
valid_chord_label.used_for: filtering notes based on the available annotations

valid_cadence_label.description: True when some of the notes in a piece come with a cadence label.
valid_cadence_label.used_for: filtering notes based on the available annotations

valid_phrase_label.description: True when some of the notes in a piece come with a phrase label.
valid_phrase_label.used_for: filtering notes based on the available annotations

valid_pedal_point_label.description: True when pedal point annotations will be available for this piece if it has any (always True for DLC).
valid_pedal_point_label.used_for: filtering notes based on the available annotations

valid_section_start_label.description: True when some of the notes in a piece come with a section label.
valid_section_start_label.used_for: filtering notes based on the available annotations

note_degree.description: A string expressing a note's position relative to the scale of the localkey. E.g., for a passage in local key of F minor, F: '1', Ab: '3', A: '#3', C: '5', Ab: '6', A: '#6', Bb: '7', B: '#'. etc.
